metadata.description: The network address-assignment and name-resolution SERVICE for a site — DHCP and/or DNS as one operated capability (they are two projections of the same allocation: a lease and its A/PTR records). Vendor mechanisms (ISC Kea, FreeIPA DNS, dnsmasq, UniFi) are providers, never the type. It is the server surface (Kea/BIND) that renders leases/reservations from the estate's Network.IPAddress records (ADR-023); dhcp-scope/dns-zone are its config surfaces.

services.description: Which address capabilities this service provides. (IPAM-as-a-distinct-service dropped — addressing is modeled as Network.IPAddress records per ADR-023; there is no adopted IPAM vocabulary and its substance is covered by dhcp-scope + the IPAddress records.)

relationships.name: served_by
relationships.description: served_by — a host running this DHCP/DNS server (Kea/BIND/FreeIPA). The service stops before its host in a reverse-topo shutdown.

relationships.name: served_by
relationships.description: served_by — a VM running this DHCP/DNS server. (One of BareMetalHost / VirtualMachine per instance.)
